hardware: c8a.4xlarge (AMD Zen5 EPYC 9R45, 16 vCPU) on-demand, ap-northeast-1
change: fixed256 funnel: queries transposed once per tile into word-planar stack groups (qw[w][j] = query j's word w), doc word broadcast against 8 lane-resident queries via AVX-512 intrinsics (vpbroadcastq+vpxorq+vpopcntq+vpaddq x4, one store). Kills the 072 ASM's per-doc invariant re-establishment: ~10 uops of fat-pointer de-interleave + 4 vpgatherqq re-fetching the same 256 B of query words every doc.

autovectorizer_detour.attempt: scalar planar loop, u64 lanes end-to-end

autovectorizer_detour.why: the reduction shape must be written as intrinsics — the autovectorizer refuses it from scalar code, even though it BUILT the same instruction sequence itself in 072 (from a shape it recognized)

gain: +18-25% over 072 at batch=8 like-for-like (1185 vs 1106 hides the real win: the knee MOVED — per-query tile state is now 32 B in shared zmm rows instead of pointer+heap machinery, so batch=32 becomes optimal at 1307). C=500 at 1542 QPS.
note: objdump confirms: zero vpgatherqq in the funnel (the 2 remaining in the binary are the itq/other bins), no vpermq pointer surgery; the loop is broadcast/xor/popcnt/add x4 + one store, query zmm rows hoisted. Tiling model: with per-doc group cost ~17 uops and per-group state 4 zmm, T* = sqrt(s/k) moved from 8 to ~32; past 64 the qw_groups array (64x256B) plus heaps spill L2 and rayon chunking gets noisy (cv 6-18%).
